title: Add podman commit command
body:
podman commit allows the user to commit containers
as images with options of tagging the image, setting
a commit message, setting the author, and making
changes to the instructions.

Signed-off-by: umohnani8 <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body: :umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 5770dc2) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
LGTM. CI seems to be having issues though.

I do not understand why the tests are failing
```
not ok 4 podman commit default

# (in test file ./podman_commit.bats, line 23)

#   `[ "$status" -eq 0 ]' failed

# 0

# Trying to pull ...Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal:latest...

# Getting image source signatures

# Copying blob sha256:1e3761769a4599f75b50d1bd2376e6897ee23d38875e0b046a418decc1b68aa8

 41.42 MB / 41.42 MB [======================================================] 2s

# Copying config sha256:4a42f64743f10e06d783370d88de03a0f71dc2556144256cdfc445fa144c008c

 1.27 KB / 1.27 KB [========================================================] 0s

# Writing manifest to image destination

# Storing signatures

# 9e2d64dcb700516f79c3bebd99a3d7aca854b6b572db67bddc53c311690229a2

# too many arguments. Usage CONTAINER [REFERENCE]

# bash: line 1: Getting: command not found

# bash: line 2: Copying: command not found

# bash: line 3: $'\r': command not found

# bash: line 4: Copying: command not found

# bash: line 5: $'\r': command not found

# bash: line 6: Writing: command not found

# bash: line 7: Storing: command not found

# bash: line 8: 9e2d64dcb700516f79c3bebd99a3d7aca854b6b572db67bddc53c311690229a2: command not found

# 9e2d64dcb700516f79c3bebd99a3d7aca854b6b572db67bddc53c311690229a2
```

I am using the right number of arguments, and when I do it manually it works fine. Any ideas @rhatdan @mheon?

I think you're getting some of our informational output instead of the container ID as $CONTAINER_ID, and then trying to feed informational messages into podman commit as container IDs. You might want to name the container with --name and then use that to commit instead of the ID.
@mheon I was using the -d flag as that runs the container and returns the container id. I will try the name method.
@umohnani8 Getting output out of podman commands is tricky, it comes with an extra \r code that needs to be stripped.  Using the --name is a much better solution.

:pushpin: Commit 81502d1 has been approved by `rhatdan`
